prompt:
You are a software requirements analyst.
You are given a list of NON-FUNCTIONAL requirements (NFRs).

Your task is to classify each requirement into exactly ONE of the following categories:

performance
security
maintainability
other

Definitions:

Performance:

Concerns measurable runtime behavior of the system, such as response time, latency, throughput, processing time, execution time, resource usage, runtime efficiency, synchronization frequency, or active simultaneous use.
Typical aspects: response time, latency, throughput, processing speed, execution time, resource consumption, runtime efficiency, synchronization within a time interval, processing duration, or the system’s ability to support active simultaneous work.
Classify a requirement as performance when its primary concern is how fast, how often, or under what runtime load the system performs an operation.
Classify as performance when the requirement clearly describes response time, processing time, synchronization time/frequency, throughput, runtime delay, or active simultaneous use of the system.

Security:

Concerns access control, authentication, authorization, data protection, confidentiality, integrity, privacy, auditability, or prevention of unauthorized actions.
Typical aspects: encryption, login, permissions, authorization, access control, confidentiality, privacy, integrity, audit trails, security-relevant logging, prevention of unauthorized access, protection from invalid or corrupted data, or protection against malicious actions.
Classify a requirement as security when its primary concern is protecting data, controlling access, preventing unauthorized use, preserving confidentiality, preserving integrity, preventing data corruption, validating data to protect correctness, or keeping audit/security records.

Maintainability:

Concerns modifiability, configurability, extensibility, code quality, ease of updates, ease of maintenance, debugging, documentation, releases, upgrades, scheduled maintenance, maintenance cost, adaptation to new technology, or system evolution.
Typical aspects: easy to modify, configurable, extensible, modular design, coding best practices, documented, testable, maintainable over time, easy to update, easy to upgrade, version releases, scheduled maintenance, maintenance budget, support for future changes, or adaptation to new technology.
Classify a requirement as maintainability when its primary concern is ease of modification, maintenance, extension, testing, debugging, documentation, updating, upgrading, release management, scheduled maintenance, adaptation to future changes, or system evolution.

Other:

Any NFR whose primary concern is something other than performance, security, or maintainability.
Typical aspects include usability, accessibility, compatibility, portability, interoperability, legal constraints, user-interface quality, deployment, operating environments, localization, licensing, distribution, availability, reliability, fault tolerance, robustness, installation, platform constraints, general scalability, general capacity planning, number of stored items, number of supported customers, or general technology/platform constraints.
Use other when the requirement describes a quality attribute or constraint that is not clearly one of the three target categories.

Important guidelines:

Choose the category that best matches the requirement's primary concern.
If a requirement contains aspects of more than one category, choose the category that represents the main quality concern.
When deciding between a target category and other, choose the target category only if the requirement clearly and primarily matches performance, security, or maintainability. Otherwise, choose other.
Do not use other when the requirement clearly expresses one of the target concerns.

Decision rule:

Determine the requirement's primary NFR concern.
Assign the category that best matches that concern.
Use performance for measurable runtime speed, timing, processing, synchronization, throughput, or active simultaneous use.
Use security for access control, data protection, confidentiality, integrity, auditability, or prevention of unauthorized or corrupting actions.
Use maintainability for modification, maintenance, updates, upgrades, releases, scheduled maintenance, maintenance cost, documentation, testing, or system evolution.
Use other when the primary concern is outside performance, security, and maintainability.

Instructions:

For each requirement, output exactly one line in the format:
REQ_ID: CATEGORY

Use only one of the four categories: performance, security, maintainability, other.
Use only the original requirement IDs from the input.
Do not include explanations, summaries, or any extra text.
Do not change the format or add punctuation.

Output format (STRICT):

R1: performance
R2: security
R3: maintainability
R4: other
...

Requirements:
1. [R374] Administrator shall be able to activate a pre-paid card via the Administration section in under 5 seconds.
2. [R375] Customer shall be able to check the status of their prepaid card  by entering in the PIN number  in under 5 seconds.
3. [R376] System shall let customers register on the website as a pay as you go user in under 5 minutes.
4. [R377] System shall let customers register on the website as a unlimited movie subscriber user in under 10 minutes.
5. [R378] System shall let existing customers log into the website with their email address and password in under 5 seconds.
6. [R379] System shall let customers cancel their account  process shall take no longer than 2 minutes.  Database will mark the account as inactive  and user will not be able to log into the website.
7. [R380] System shall let administrator de-activate a customer account in under 1 minute. Customer will no longer be able to access the website.
8. [R381] System shall let administrator add/remove movies on the website in under 5 minutes.  Entered movie information will be stored in the database and will now be available on the website.
9. [R382] System shall let the administrator add/remove categories on the website in under 5 minutes.
10. [R383] System shall let Izogn Manager access sales and usage statistics in under 2 minutes.
11. [R384] System shall let administrators generate unique pre-paid pins in under 2 minutes and generate an Excel file with the generated PINs.
12. [R385] The website shall be attractive to all audiences.  The website shall appear to be fun and the colors should be bright and vibrant.
13. [R386] The website should have an African feel  but should not alienate non-Africans.  The website should use animation on pages which are describing the services  to grab the users attention and encourage them to sign up.
14. [R387] The website design should be modern  clean  and concise.
15. [R388] The product should be able to be used by 90% of novice users on the Internet.
16. [R389] Users should only have to navigate through a maximum of 4 pages  to create an account with the website.
17. [R390] Users should be able to access their streaming movies in under 2 clicks after logging into the website.
18. [R391] An anonymous survey will show that 70% of customers are comfortable purchasing streaming movies after 2 weeks using the website.
19. [R392] An anonymous survey will show that 95% of customers are comfortable using and purchasing pre-paid cards after a month of using the system.
20. [R393] The product shall retain the customers buying preferences and information.
21. [R394] The product shall be easy for customers with novice skills on the  Internet to purchase and watch streaming movies.  Customers should be able to purchase and watch a pre-determined movie in under 3 minutes.
22. [R395] The product shall use symbols and words that are naturally understandable by users familiar with websites
23. [R396] The website will use standard Internet conventions when interacting with the user.
24. [R397] Any interface between a user and the automated system shall have a maximum response time of 5 seconds  unless noted by an exception below.
25. [R398] When purchasing a streaming movie or pre-paid card via credit card  the processing time should have a maximum response time of 15 seconds.
26. [R399] When streaming a movie  the buffering time should take no longer than 10 seconds (plus any latency on the users connection.)
27. [R400] While streaming a movie  buffering should only occur if there is latency in the users connection.
28. [R401] All credit card information will be secured on the server  and only accessible by authorized Izogn administrators.  Information will be encrypted in the database.
29. [R402] The website shall be available for use 24 hours per day  365 days per year.
30. [R403] The website shall achieve 99.5% up time.
31. [R404] All movies shall be streamed on demand  at any time of the day.
32. [R405] The website shall continue to operate if the streaming server goes down.
33. [R406] The website shall continue to operate if the payment gateway goes down.
34. [R407] The streaming server will support 50 simultaneous streaming connections.
35. [R408] The streaming server will support a maximum of 700 movies; new servers can be added to support more movies.
36. [R409] The server will support a maximum of 1 000 simultaneous users.
37. [R410] The website shall be capable of supporting 100 000 customers.
38. [R411] The product shall be expected to operate within the maximum maintenance budget for a minimum of 2 years.
39. [R412] All aspects of the website shall be accessed by a web browser over the Internet.
40. [R413] The same hardware supporting the current website will be used to support the new system.
41. [R414] We must be able to interface with the following browsers: IE 5.X  6.0  Netscape 6.x  7.x  8.x and Firefox 1.0.
42. [R415] Website must interface with the CyberSource API to process credit card transactions.
43. [R416] Website must be fully operational with MSN TV2.
44. [R417] Movies shall be encoded and streamed via Windows Media Player 9.0.
45. [R418] Movies shall be encoded for 56k and 256k streams.
46. [R419] Promotional updates to the website should take a day to update.
47. [R420] Izogn Administrator must be able to update the category listings on the website within 2 minutes.
48. [R421] Izogn Administrator must be able to add new products to the website within 2 minutes.
49. [R422] The system will provide a 24 hour 800 toll free number for support to its customers.
50. [R423] The website will provide a help section with answers and solutions to common problems.
51. [R424] Velkat staff will be available for phone support between 7am-7pm  M-F to provide technical support to the Izogn administrators.
52. [R425] The product shall be able to be installed in the specified environment within 1 working day.
53. [R426] Only registered customers can purchase streaming movies.
54. [R427] A customer can only stream a movie if they purchased it  and are within the 2 day viewing period.
55. [R428] Only authorized personnel can access customer records in the database.
56. [R429] Only administrators can activate a pre-paid card.
57. [R430] Only authorized personnel can access sales information.
58. [R431] The website shall prevent its data from being corrupted when incorrect data is entered in.
59. [R432] The website shall protect itself from intentional abuse  and notify the administrator at all occurrences.
60. [R433] Data integrity scripts will be run on a weekly basis to verify the integrity of the database.
61. [R434] The website shall make its user aware of its information practices before collection data from them via a Privacy Policy  accessible on all pages of the website.
62. [R435] The system shall notify customers of changes to its information policy.
63. [R436] The system shall reveal private information only in compliance with the organizations information policy.
64. [R437] All customer information will be stored on a secure database  accessible only to authorized personnel.
65. [R438] All credit card information will be encrypted in the database.
66. [R439] The streaming server will have anti-virus software to prevent infection of malicious viruses.
67. [R440] The website should cater to all tribes in Nigeria.
68. [R441] The website shall not be offensive to religious or ethnic groups.
69. [R442] The website should appeal to all Africans  not only Nigerians.
70. [R443] The website should use Flash to provide animation on the home page.
71. [R444] IzognMovies shall meet the licensing requirements set forth by the appropriate organizations in order to stream African movies.
72. [R445] The website will comply with W3C standards.
73. [R446] The PHP code will comply with PEAR standards.
